---
title: "GAD-7 Assessment Data Cleaning"
author: "Cristin Young"
date: "2026-03-17"
format:
  html:
    df-print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-float: true
---

## Project Overview

This notebook prepares **CYBHI EBP/CDEP** Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 (GAD-7) assessment data for analysis and reporting. It ingests the raw eInsight export, processes the two-row hierarchical headers, maps text responses to numeric values, computes total scores and cut-off bands, pairs each client's initial assessment with their last follow-up, and writes a clean paired output.

**Data source:** `GAD-7.csv` — eInsight Individual Assessments export.

**Scoring:**

-   7 items. Responses are stored as text and mapped to numeric values: Not at all = 0, Several days = 1, More than half the days = 2, Nearly every day = 3.
-   Total score range: 0–21. Higher scores indicate greater anxiety severity.
-   **A lower follow-up score indicates a positive outcome.**
-   Total score is set to `NA` if 2 or more items are missing.
-   Cut-off bands: 0–4 = minimal, 5–9 = mild, 10–14 = moderate, 15–21 = severe.
-   Item 8 (functional impairment) is not included in the total score.

**Pairing logic:** Clients are grouped by `client_system_id`. The first assessment is the initial; the last assessment (if ≥2 exist) is the follow-up. Clients with only one assessment are excluded.

**Output:**

| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `gad7_paired_clean.csv` | One row per client: initial/follow-up scores and bands, `positive_outcome`, `score_change`, quarter labels |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Open Issues / Action Items

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Setup

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(zoo)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Helper Function

```{r header-helper}
# Reads an eInsight assessment CSV export with two-row headers.
# Returns a cleaned data frame with combined column names and empty columns removed.
read_assessment_csv <- function(path, na_vals = c("-", "", "NA", "N/A")) {
  raw <- read_csv(path, na = na_vals, col_names = FALSE, show_col_types = FALSE,
                  col_types = cols(.default = "c"))

  h1    <- unlist(raw[1, ], use.names = FALSE)
  h2    <- unlist(raw[2, ], use.names = FALSE)
  h1_ff <- na.locf(h1, na.rm = FALSE) |> str_squish()

  new_names <- ifelse(
    is.na(h1_ff) | h1_ff == "",
    h2,
    paste(h1_ff, h2, sep = " - ")
  )
  new_names <- janitor::make_clean_names(new_names)

  df <- raw[-c(1, 2), ]
  names(df) <- new_names

  df |> select(where(~ !all(is.na(.))))
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Read and Score

```{r gad7-read}
gad7_raw <- read_assessment_csv(
  "/Users/cristin/CWS Dropbox/Cristin Young/CYBHI Project/Data/eInsight Exports/Individual Assessments/GAD-7.csv"
)

response_cols <- names(gad7_raw)[
  endsWith(names(gad7_raw), "_response") &
  !str_detect(names(gad7_raw), "if_you_checked")
]

response_map <- c(
  "Not at all"              = 0,
  "Several days"            = 1,
  "More than half the days" = 2,
  "Nearly every day"        = 3
)

gad7_scores <- gad7_raw |>
  mutate(across(all_of(response_cols), ~ as.numeric(response_map[.]))) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    missing_count = sum(is.na(c_across(all_of(response_cols)))),
    total_score   = if_else(
      missing_count >= 2,
      NA_real_,
      sum(c_across(all_of(response_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    cut_off_band = case_when(
      total_score <= 4                        ~ "minimal",
      total_score >= 5  & total_score <= 9   ~ "mild",
      total_score >= 10 & total_score <= 14  ~ "moderate",
      total_score >= 15                       ~ "severe",
      .default = NA_character_
    ),
    date_completed = as.Date(date_completed)
  ) |>
  filter(
    date_completed >= "2024-01-01",
    date_completed <= Sys.Date()
  ) |>
  select(client_system_id, collection, date_completed, total_score, cut_off_band)

cat("GAD-7 rows after scoring:", nrow(gad7_scores), "\n")
cat("Unique clients:          ", n_distinct(gad7_scores$client_system_id), "\n")
cat("Missing total_score:     ", sum(is.na(gad7_scores$total_score)), "\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 2. Pair Initial and Follow-up

```{r gad7-pair}
gad7_paired <- gad7_scores |>
  group_by(client_system_id) |>
  arrange(date_completed, .by_group = TRUE) |>
  summarise(
    n_assessments  = n(),
    initial_date   = first(date_completed),
    initial_score  = first(total_score),
    initial_band   = first(cut_off_band),
    follow_up_date  = if_else(n_assessments >= 2, last(date_completed),  as.Date(NA)),
    follow_up_score = if_else(n_assessments >= 2, last(total_score),     NA_real_),
    follow_up_band  = if_else(n_assessments >= 2, last(cut_off_band),    NA_character_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(follow_up_date)) |>
  mutate(
    days_since_initial = as.integer(follow_up_date - initial_date),
    # Lower follow-up = positive outcome (lower anxiety severity is better)
    # 1 = improved, 0 = no change, -1 = worsened
    positive_outcome = case_when(
      follow_up_score <  initial_score ~ 1L,
      follow_up_score == initial_score ~ 0L,
      follow_up_score >  initial_score ~ -1L
    ),
    score_change      = follow_up_score - initial_score,
    initial_quarter   = paste0("Q", quarter(initial_date),    " ", year(initial_date)),
    follow_up_quarter = paste0("Q", quarter(follow_up_date), " ", year(follow_up_date))
  )

cat("GAD-7 paired clients:", nrow(gad7_paired), "\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 3. Quick Check

```{r gad7-check}
gad7_paired |>
  summarise(
    n_paired          = n(),
    n_improved        = sum(positive_outcome ==  1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_no_change       = sum(positive_outcome ==  0, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_worsened        = sum(positive_outcome == -1, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_improved      = round(100 * n_improved / n_paired, 1),
    n_missing_outcome = sum(is.na(positive_outcome))
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 4. Write Clean CSV

```{r write-csv}
out_dir <- "/Users/cristin/CWS Dropbox/Cristin Young/CYBHI Project/Rebecca and Tristan and Cristin/EInsight Data"

write_csv(gad7_paired, file.path(out_dir, "gad7_paired_clean.csv"))

cat("CSV written successfully.\n")
```
